publisherName: City of South Bend Water Works
claimSummary: South Bend Water Works says residents should use its service line inventory map to check address status, complete the city service line survey to verify private-side material, and use 311 or water quality staff for free lead testing and follow-up.
reviewerNote: Official South Bend Water Works page reviewed manually for map, survey, private-side verification, and testing workflow.

publisherName: City of South Bend Water Works
claimSummary: South Bend says more than 34000 customers were asked to complete the service line material survey for EPA inventory and mapping requirements and that 311 or the water quality team remains the help path while private-side verification continues before the city overstates replacement timing.
reviewerNote: Official South Bend utility notice and survey article reviewed manually for survey scale, ongoing verification, and resident help language.

claimSummary: Lafayette says about 3200 service lines are currently flagged as lead galvanized requiring replacement or unknown and publishes an online database showing address details for current project areas.
reviewerNote: Official Lafayette replacement program page reviewed manually for utility inventory and project-area details.

claimSummary: Lafayette says letters were mailed in early November 2024 to residents whose service lines are lead galvanized requiring replacement or unknown and posts both the notice copies and 45-day construction notice workflow online.
reviewerNote: Official Lafayette resident notice language reviewed manually for mailed notice and construction notice workflow.

claimSummary: Lafayette says identified lead service lines in current project areas are coordinated for replacement at no cost through grants and loans and that post-replacement water testing will be performed.
reviewerNote: Official Lafayette program page reviewed manually for project-area replacement details.

claimSummary: Lafayette says phase I replacement work is funded through grants and loans with no cost to customers in the active project areas, but the no-cost claim is tied to those scheduled areas rather than every address citywide.
reviewerNote: Official Lafayette cost coverage language reviewed manually with project-area caveat.

claimSummary: Mishawaka says it has already replaced the utility-owned portion of about 4100 lead service lines and continues field investigations plus predictive modeling to identify remaining unknown lines.
reviewerNote: Official Mishawaka lead free page reviewed manually for replacement progress and inventory details.

claimSummary: Mishawaka says letters were mailed in October or November 2024 to customers with lead galvanized steel or unknown service lines and that the utility-side segment will be replaced within 45 days when an owner replaces a lead or galvanized private-side line.
reviewerNote: Official Mishawaka notice and inspection guidance reviewed manually for mailed letters and 45-day coordination language.

claimSummary: Bloomington says it is potholing properties to verify private-side material, updating its inventory with field work, and publishing the results through an interactive map and dashboard with lead GRR unknown and non-lead categories.
reviewerNote: Official Bloomington inventory page reviewed manually for potholing and map-dashboard workflow.

claimSummary: Bloomington says residents can use an interactive map and dashboard to see lead non-lead galvanized requiring replacement and unknown line types and that block-by-block replacement planning is underway for work beginning in 2027.
reviewerNote: Official Bloomington replacement project page reviewed manually for map and 2027 planning context.

claimSummary: Bloomington says the lead service line replacement project will replace outdated lead water lines on a block-by-block basis beginning in 2027 under the city's rate-funded replacement plan.
reviewerNote: Official Bloomington program page reviewed manually for block-by-block 2027 replacement timing.

claimSummary: Bloomington says customer-side replacement costs beginning in 2027 are expected to be covered by recent water rate increases with no additional cost to the property owner, though the city does not guarantee funds will be sufficient in every case.
reviewerNote: Official Bloomington cost language reviewed manually for rate-funded no-additional-cost expectations and caveat.

publisherName: City of Aurora Water Production Division
claimSummary: Aurora says residents can use its interactive lead service line portal to verify whether a line is lead unknown galvanized or connected through connector-unknown material.
reviewerNote: Official Aurora lead notice portal reviewed manually for inventory and map details.

publisherName: City of Aurora Water Production Division
claimSummary: Aurora says it issued a citywide 2025 lead action level exceedance notice, explained the new sampling rule changes, and directed residents to mitigation steps plus the service line portal.
reviewerNote: Official Aurora lead notice portal reviewed manually for action-level notice language.

publisherName: City of Aurora Water Production Division
claimSummary: Aurora says customers can request replacement through the lead notice portal, the city replaces lines at no cost when disturbances elevated lead results or other city triggers qualify, and annual replacement opportunities expanded beginning in 2026.
reviewerNote: Official Aurora replacement progress page reviewed manually for qualifying replacement paths.

publisherName: City of Aurora Water Production Division
claimSummary: Aurora says the city replaces qualifying lead service line work at no cost when disturbances or elevated sampling results occur, while owner-managed early replacements still require the homeowner to pay for the private-side segment and the city completes the remainder at no cost within 30 days.
reviewerNote: Official Aurora cost responsibility language reviewed manually.
